## Restored deliverable

### `EXPERIMENT.md`

````markdown
# EXPERIMENT: Validating Forward-Euler Integration in DiffBot's Hardware Interface

## 1. System Under Test (SUT)

The integrator under test is the loop body in
`example_2/hardware/diffbot_system.cpp:192`:

```cpp
hw_positions_[i] = hw_positions_[i] + period.seconds() * hw_velocities_[i];
```

inside `DiffBotSystemHardware::read()` (lines 181â€“204). The companion
`write()` (lines 206â€“224) mirrors commands directly into the velocity state:
`hw_velocities_[i] = hw_commands_[i]`. The integrator therefore consumes the
velocity that was set by the **previous** `write()` (the controller_manager
order is `read â†’ update â†’ write`, so the velocity state used by `read()` at
cycle k is the command issued at cycle kâˆ’1).

Relevant fixed parameters that ground the predicted bound:

| Parameter | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `controller_manager.update_rate` | **10 Hz** â†’ Î”t = 0.100 s | `example_2/bringup/config/diffbot_controllers.yaml:3` |
| `wheel_radius` | 0.015 m | `diffbot_controllers.yaml:18` |
| `wheel_separation` | 0.10 m | `diffbot_controllers.yaml:16` |
| Controller type | `diff_drive_controller/DiffDriveController` | `diffbot_controllers.yaml:9` |
| Controller `publish_rate` (odom & TF) | 50 Hz | `diffbot_controllers.yaml:24` |
| `linear.x.max_acceleration` | 1.0 m/sÂ² | `diffbot_controllers.yaml:43` |
| `linear.x.max_velocity` | 1.0 m/s | `diffbot_controllers.yaml:42` |
| `cmd_vel_timeout` | 0.5 s | `diffbot_controllers.yaml:33` |
| `open_loop` | true (odom is fed by controller-side integration of cmd, **not** by joint_states) | `diffbot_controllers.yaml:30` |

Both joints (`left_wheel_joint`, `right_wheel_joint`) declare
`command_interface=velocity` and `state_interface=position,velocity` in
`example_2/description/ros2_control/diffbot.ros2_control.xacro:21â€“30`.

Bring-up: `ros2 launch ros2_control_demo_example_2 diffbot.launch.py`
(default args: `gui:=true`, `use_mock_hardware:=false`,
`disable_commands:=false`). **`use_mock_hardware` MUST be `false`** so that
the C++ integrator in `diffbot_system.cpp` is exercised; setting it to true
substitutes `mock_components/GenericSystem` (xacro line 16) which we are
**not** testing.

## 2. Hypothesis (falsifiable, quantitative)

For each wheel joint *i*, given a constant commanded wheel angular velocity
`Ï‰_cmd_i` (rad/s) held for an interval of length `T` (s) starting after the
controller's velocity-ramp transient, the change in the joint's reported
position state is

> **H1 (point form):** `Î”q_i(T) = Ï‰_cmd_i Â· T`, with absolute error bounded
> by `|Î”q_i(T) âˆ’ Ï‰_cmd_i Â· T| â‰¤ |Ï‰_cmd_i| Â· Î”t + Îµ_jitter`, where
> Î”t = 0.100 s (one controller period at 10 Hz update rate) and
> Îµ_jitter â‰¤ 0.5 Â· |Ï‰_cmd_i| Â· Î”t accounts for the fact that
> /joint_states is sampled by `joint_state_broadcaster` asynchronously
> from `read()`.

> **H2 (slope form):** Across a sweep of constant commands, regressing
> `Î”q_i(T)` (after subtracting the modeled ramp displacement, see Â§4.2) on
> `Ï‰_cmd_i Â· T` yields slope **1.0 Â± 0.02** and intercept
> **0 Â± (Î”t Â· Ï‰_cmd_max) = Â± 0.667 rad** for the largest level used,
> with RÂ² â‰¥ 0.999.

> **H3 (residual form):** The cycle-by-cycle increment, computed from
> consecutive `/joint_states` samples whose timestamps span a single
> controller period of nominal duration Î”t, satisfies
> `(q[k] âˆ’ q[kâˆ’1]) / (t[k] âˆ’ t[kâˆ’1])  â‰ˆ Ï‰_state_i[k]`
> with mean absolute residual â‰¤ 1 % of `|Ï‰_cmd_i|`. This directly probes
> the line `hw_positions_[i] += period.seconds() * hw_velocities_[i]`.

A **refutation** is any of: a slope outside [0.98, 1.02], a residual that
grows super-linearly with `T` (indicating drift not predicted by Euler), a
dependence of integrated position on a confound held constant in Â§3.2, or
a per-cycle residual mean > 5 % of `|Ï‰_cmd_i|` that cannot be explained
by sampling jitter (Â§5).

## 3. Experimental Design

### 3.1 Independent variables

| IV | Levels | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Commanded wheel angular velocity** `Ï‰_cmd` | Derived from `cmd_vel.linear.x âˆˆ {0.05, 0.10, 0.25, 0.50} m/s` (with `angular.z = 0`) â‡’ `Ï‰ = v/r âˆˆ {3.33, 6.67, 16.67, 33.33} rad/s` | Spans an order of magnitude; all within `linear.x.max_velocity = 1.0 m/s`. |
| **Hold duration** `T` | {2, 5, 10, 20} s | Spans 20Ã—â€“200Ã— one controller period (Î”t = 0.1 s); shows whether errors grow with `T` (they should not, beyond the bounded one-step lag). |
| **Direction** | {forward, reverse} via sign of `linear.x` | Symmetry check: integrator should be sign-agnostic. |

Total runs: 4 Ã— 4 Ã— 2 = 32, plus 3 replicates per cell â†’ **96 runs**.
Each run is independent: between runs, send `linear.x = 0` and wait
`cmd_vel_timeout = 0.5 s` before the next command, then reset the position
baseline by reading the latest `/joint_states` value (the integrator never
zeroes after activation, so we use **change** in position, not absolute).

### 3.2 Confounds held constant (and how)

| Confound | Control |
|---|---|
| Pure rotation of the base (would give opposing wheel signs) | Always set `cmd_vel.angular.z = 0`; verify in recorded `/cmd_vel`. |
| Controller velocity ramp (`linear.x.max_acceleration = 1.0 m/sÂ²`) | Either (a) **subtract** the closed-form ramp displacement (see Â§4.2), or (b) start logging `T` seconds **after** issuing the command, where ramp is complete (`t_ramp = v/a_max â‰¤ 0.5 s` for the largest level). We use (b) for primary analysis and (a) as cross-check. |
| `controller_manager.update_rate` | Leave at the YAML default 10 Hz. Record the actual `period.seconds()` indirectly via consecutive `/joint_states` deltas; flag any cycle whose duration deviates from 0.100 s by > 20 %. |
| Other CPU load | Run on an otherwise idle machine; close RViz by launching with `gui:=false`. |
| Initial joint state | Each run records baseline `q_0` from `/joint_states` immediately before the command transition; report only `Î”q = q(T) âˆ’ q_0`. |
| Floating-point precision (running over very long T) | Cap per-run `T â‰¤ 20 s` and `|Ï‰_cmd| â‰¤ 33.33 rad/s` â‡’ |q| â‰¤ 667 rad, well inside double-precision exactness. |
| Controller telemetry path | `open_loop: true` (yaml line 30) means `/diffbot_base_controller/odom` is **not** derived from `/joint_states`; we therefore use **`/joint_states` only** as the integrator output. We still record odom as a sanity reference. |

## 4. Procedure

### 4.1 Bring-up (one shell each)

```bash
# Terminal 1: launch the stack with the real (C++) hardware interface
ros2 launch ros2_control_demo_example_2 diffbot.launch.py \
    gui:=false use_mock_hardware:=false

# Terminal 2: confirm controllers loaded
ros2 control list_controllers
# expect: joint_state_broadcaster active, diffbot_base_controller active

# Terminal 3: confirm the right plugin is loaded (NOT mock_components)
ros2 control list_hardware_components
# expect: ros2_control_demo_example_2/DiffBotSystemHardware
```

### 4.2 Per-run protocol

For each (Ï‰_cmd, T, direction) cell:

1. Start a bag (see Â§5) named `run_<v>_<T>_<dir>_<rep>.bag`.
2. Wait 1 s with no command, then publish `cmd_vel` at 20 Hz:
   ```bash
   ros2 topic pub -r 20 /cmd_vel geometry_msgs/msg/TwistStamped \
     "{header: {frame_id: ''}, twist: {linear: {x: <v>}, angular: {z: 0.0}}}"
   ```
   (Note: `/diffbot_base_controller/cmd_vel` is remapped to `/cmd_vel` in
   `diffbot.launch.py:91`. `diff_drive_controller` accepts
   `TwistStamped` as of current ros2_controllers.)
3. Hold for `t_ramp + T + 0.5 s`, where `t_ramp = |v|/1.0 s`.
4. Stop the publisher; publish a single `linear.x = 0` to halt.
5. Wait `cmd_vel_timeout + 1 s = 1.5 s` for the controller to quiesce.
6. Stop the bag.

### 4.3 Reduction

For each run, locate two `/joint_states` samples:
- `t_0`: first sample with timestamp â‰¥ command-issue + `t_ramp`
- `t_1`: last sample with timestamp â‰¤ `t_0 + T`

Then `T_eff = t_1 âˆ’ t_0`, `Î”q_i = q_i(t_1) âˆ’ q_i(t_0)`, expected
`Î”qÌ‚_i = Ï‰_cmd_i Â· T_eff` with `Ï‰_cmd_i = sign(v) Â· |v| / r`.

Cross-check (a) with the ramp included: integrate the closed-form ramp
displacement `0.5 Â· vÂ² / a_max Â· (1/r)` from command-issue to `t_0` and
verify it matches the observed displacement during the ramp window.

## 5. Topics to Record

Use `ros2 bag record` with `--qos-profile-overrides-path` only if needed.
All topics below are published by the default bring-up.

| Topic | Type | Source / publish rate | Why we record it |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/joint_states` | `sensor_msgs/JointState` | `joint_state_broadcaster`, by default at the controller_manager `update_rate` (10 Hz) | **Primary integrator output.** Provides `position[i]` and `velocity[i]` from `hw_positions_` / `hw_velocities_` in `diffbot_system.cpp`. |
| `/dynamic_joint_states` | `control_msgs/DynamicJointState` | `joint_state_broadcaster`, 10 Hz | Independent confirmation that joint values match `/joint_states` (helps catch broadcaster bugs). |
| `/cmd_vel` | `geometry_msgs/TwistStamped` | published by us at 20 Hz | Records the actual command timing and value, including the precise stop transition. |
| `/diffbot_base_controller/odom` | `nav_msgs/Odometry` | `diff_drive_controller`, 50 Hz (yaml line 24). With `open_loop: true`, this is integrated from **commands**, not from joint_states. | Sanity check: odom-derived linear distance should equal `r Â· mean(Î”q_left, Î”q_right)` only after the ramp; a mismatch flags a `cmd_vel`-ingestion bug, not an integrator bug. |
| `/tf` | `tf2_msgs/TFMessage` | `diff_drive_controller`, 50 Hz | Same as odom; secondary sanity. |
| `/rosout` | `rcl_interfaces/Log` | event-driven | Captures the `RCLCPP_INFO_THROTTLE` lines from `read()`/`write()` (every 500 ms; `diffbot_system.cpp:200,220`) for post-hoc cross-check. |

Recording command:

```bash
ros2 bag record -o run_<id> \
    /joint_states /dynamic_joint_states /cmd_vel \
    /diffbot_base_controller/odom /tf /rosout
```

Sample-rate rationale: 10 Hz on `/joint_states` is the **maximum useful
rate** for the hypothesis because the underlying integrator only fires once
per controller period. Recording at higher rates would oversample without
providing new integrator state. We record `/cmd_vel` at 20 Hz to bracket
each control cycle with at least one command sample so the effective
command-on time is reconstructable to â‰¤ 50 ms.

## 6. Quantitative Success Thresholds

A run-cell **passes** if all of the following hold (units explicit):

| Metric | Threshold | Units |
|---|---|---|
| Per-cell mean `Î”q âˆ’ Ï‰_cmd Â· T_eff` | within `[âˆ’|Ï‰_cmd| Â· Î”t, +|Ï‰_cmd| Â· Î”t]` = `[âˆ’0.1Â·Ï‰_cmd, +0.1Â·Ï‰_cmd]` | rad |
| Per-cell std-dev across 3 replicates | â‰¤ `0.5 Â· |Ï‰_cmd| Â· Î”t` = `0.05 Â· |Ï‰_cmd|` | rad |
| Symmetry: `|Î”q_forward + Î”q_reverse|` for matched (|v|, T) | â‰¤ `0.1 Â· |Ï‰_cmd| Â· Î”t` | rad |
| Slope of `Î”q` vs `Ï‰_cmd Â· T_eff` regression (all 96 runs) | 1.000 Â± 0.02 | dimensionless |
| Regression intercept | within Â±0.667 rad (= Î”t Â· Ï‰_cmd_max) | rad |
| Regression RÂ² | â‰¥ 0.999 | dimensionless |
| Mean per-cycle residual `\|(q[k]âˆ’q[kâˆ’1])/(t[k]âˆ’t[kâˆ’1]) âˆ’ Ï‰_state[k]\|` | â‰¤ `0.01 Â· |Ï‰_cmd|` | rad/s |
| Î”t observed: median of `t[k]âˆ’t[kâˆ’1]` on `/joint_states` | 0.100 s Â± 20 % | s |

H1/H2/H3 are **refuted** if any threshold above is violated outside
sampling-jitter explanation (i.e., the violation does not shrink when
runs are filtered to cycles whose `t[k]âˆ’t[kâˆ’1]` is within 5 % of 0.100 s).

## 7. Visualization Plan

Produce the following plots from the bagged data (e.g., with
`rosbag2_py` + matplotlib, one notebook per analysis):

1. **Per-run trajectory** (one figure per run):
   - x-axis: time since command issue (s)
   - y-axis (left): `q_left`, `q_right` from `/joint_states` (rad)
   - y-axis (right, twin): commanded `v` from `/cmd_vel` (m/s)
   - Overlay: dashed line `qÌ‚(t) = Ï‰_cmd Â· max(0, t âˆ’ t_ramp)` shifted by
     `q_0`. **Inspect:** the dashed line should sit on top of the data
     after `t_ramp`; deviation visible to the eye refutes H1.

2. **Slope plot** (one figure across all 96 runs):
   - x-axis: `Ï‰_cmd Â· T_eff` (rad)
   - y-axis: measured `Î”q` (rad)
   - One color per `|v|` level, one marker per direction.
   - Overlay: y = x line, fitted regression, Â±`Î”t Â· |Ï‰_cmd|` error band.
   - **Inspect:** points hug y = x; band envelopes all points; fit slope
     and RÂ² match thresholds in Â§6.

3. **Per-cycle residual histogram**:
   - Histogram of `(Î”q_per_cycle / Î”t_per_cycle) âˆ’ Ï‰_state` across all
     cycles in all runs, faceted by `|Ï‰_cmd|` level.
   - **Inspect:** distribution centered at 0 with tails inside the
     Â±5 %Â·|Ï‰_cmd| band. A bimodal or skewed distribution suggests a
     systematic phase error between `/joint_states` and `read()`.

4. **Cycle period plot**:
   - Time series of `t[k] âˆ’ t[kâˆ’1]` for `/joint_states`.
   - **Inspect:** flat at ~0.100 s with bounded jitter; spikes correlate
     with system load (cross-reference `/rosout`).

5. **Open-loop odometry vs. wheel-integrated distance**:
   - Plot `odom.pose.position.x` vs. `r Â· 0.5Â·(q_left + q_right)`.
   - With `open_loop: true`, both come from integrating commands (one
     pre-controller, one post-hardware), so they should agree after the
     ramp. **Inspect:** divergence here implicates the controller stack,
     not the integrator under test, and is grounds for re-running with
     mitigations from Â§8.

## 8. Failure Modes & Mitigations

1. **Velocity ramp masks the test.** The `diff_drive_controller`'s
   `linear.x.max_acceleration = 1.0 m/sÂ²` (yaml line 43) means the wheel
   command is *not* a step. If we naively compare `Î”q` to `Ï‰_cmd Â· T`
   over the **full** command interval, we will see a constant negative
   offset of `0.5 Â· Ï‰_cmdÂ² Â· r / a_max`.
   - *Mitigation:* trim the analysis window to start at
     `t_0 = t_cmd + t_ramp` (Â§4.2), and additionally cross-check by
     subtracting the closed-form ramp displacement. Independently, a
     run with `v = 0.05 m/s` has `t_ramp = 0.05 s < Î”t`, so the ramp is
     contained in a single cycle and serves as a baseline.

2. **`/joint_states` publish rate is not what we think.**
   `joint_state_broadcaster`'s effective rate equals
   `controller_manager.update_rate` only if no `publish_rate` is set on
   the broadcaster (none is, in the yaml). If a future change adds one,
   the cycle-period plot in Â§7.4 will not be flat at 0.1 s.
   - *Mitigation:* the success thresholds use `T_eff` measured from
     observed timestamps, never the nominal `T`. The cycle-period plot
     is a gating check; if the median Î”t is not within 20 % of 0.100 s,
     halt and reconfigure before proceeding.

3. **`cmd_vel_timeout` (0.5 s) zeros the command mid-run.** If our
   publisher rate (20 Hz) drops below 2 Hz for any reason (CPU spike,
   ROS DDS hiccup), the controller will set `v = 0`, the integrator
   will silently produce a flat segment, and the slope test will fail
   in a way that looks like an integrator bug.
   - *Mitigation:* always publish at â‰¥ 20 Hz (10Ã— the timeout floor),
     and overlay `/cmd_vel` on the per-run trajectory plot (Â§7.1) so
     any zero gap is visually obvious. Reject runs with command gaps
     > 100 ms.

4. **Wrong hardware plugin loaded (silent).** If `use_mock_hardware:=true`
   is left set (e.g., in a saved launch file), the integrator under
   test never runs; instead `mock_components/GenericSystem` (with
   `calculate_dynamics: true`, see
   `diffbot.ros2_control.xacro:14â€“20`) provides similar-looking output
   and the experiment trivially "passes" while testing nothing.
   - *Mitigation:* every session, run `ros2 control list_hardware_components`
     and grep for `DiffBotSystemHardware`; abort if not present. Also
     scrape `/rosout` for the throttled `"Reading states:"` log line
     emitted from `diffbot_system.cpp:200`, which is unique to this
     plugin.

5. **TwistStamped vs Twist message-type mismatch.** Older
   `diff_drive_controller` builds subscribed to `geometry_msgs/Twist`;
   current builds expect `TwistStamped`. A mismatch means commands are
   silently dropped and the integrator sees zero velocity.
   - *Mitigation:* before the first run, send one test command and
     inspect `/diffbot_base_controller/odom` for non-zero
     `twist.twist.linear.x`; if zero, switch to `Twist` (no header
     field) in the publish command. Type can also be confirmed with
     `ros2 topic info -v /cmd_vel`.

6. **Position state never gets initialized (NaN).** `on_activate()`
   (`diffbot_system.cpp:147â€“156`) initializes positions to 0 only if
   they are NaN. If `on_activate` is somehow re-entered after a
   non-NaN value, positions retain their old values â€” usually fine, but
   means `Î”q` semantics depend on never trusting absolute `q`.
   - *Mitigation:* the protocol only ever uses `Î”q = q(t_1) âˆ’ q(t_0)`
     (Â§4.3); absolute position is reported but never compared to a
     constant. Additionally, log `/joint_states` for 1 s before each
     command to confirm `q` is finite and constant before we move.
````

## Judge rationales

### Trial 1 (overall_recomputed=3.00, judge_reported=3)

The plan excels across all dimensions. Hypothesis_statement provides three quantitative, falsifiable forms (point, slope, residual) with an error bound |Î”q âˆ’ Ï‰Â·T| â‰¤ |Ï‰|Â·Î”t grounded in the actual 10 Hz controller_manager update_rate from diffbot_controllers.yaml. Controlled_variables specify concrete levels with a thorough confounds table including the velocity ramp, cmd_vel_timeout, and open_loop odom path. Recorded_signals justify each topic with rate and purpose, and success_thresholds derive numeric bounds (with units) directly from the hypothesis. The visualization_plan enumerates five distinct figures with specific signals and inspection criteria, and failure_modes lists six concrete pitfalls (ramp masking, wrong hardware plugin, TwistStamped mismatch, NaN init, etc.) each paired with a specific mitigation. Repo_grounding is exemplary, citing the exact Euler line in diffbot_system.cpp:192, the on_activate NaN init, throttled log lines, yaml line numbers, and the xacro mock-vs-real plugin distinction.

### Trial 2 (overall_recomputed=3.00, judge_reported=3)

The hypothesis_statement is fully quantitative with an explicit error bound tied to the controller's 10 Hz update period (Î”t = 0.1 s), and is broken into point/slope/residual forms with explicit refutation criteria. controlled_variables enumerates concrete IV levels (v, T, direction) plus a thorough confounds table covering the velocity ramp, update rate, cmd_vel_timeout, CPU load, and floating-point precision with hold-constant strategies. recorded_signals lists each topic with type, source, rate, and per-topic rationale, and explains why 10 Hz on /joint_states is the correct ceiling. success_thresholds are numeric with units and derived directly from the hypothesis (slope 1.000 Â± 0.02, RÂ² â‰¥ 0.999, residual bands of Î”tÂ·|Ï‰|), the visualization_plan specifies five distinct plots with signals and inspection criteria, and failure_modes enumerates six concrete pitfalls (ramp, broadcaster rate, timeout, mock-hardware confusion, TwistStamped mismatch, NaN init) each with specific mitigations. repo_grounding cites exact file paths, line numbers, YAML keys, the read()/write() Euler step, hw_positions_/hw_velocities_, and open_loop:true semantics, demonstrating the plan is built on this specific runtime.

### Trial 3 (overall_recomputed=3.00, judge_reported=3)

The plan provides three quantitative, falsifiable hypotheses (point/slope/residual forms) explicitly grounded in the 10 Hz update rate from diffbot_controllers.yaml, satisfying hypothesis_statement and success_thresholds with units derived from Î”tÂ·|Ï‰|. Controlled_variables enumerate concrete IV levels plus a thorough confounds table (ramp, update_rate, cmd_vel_timeout, open_loop telemetry, FP precision). Recorded_signals justify each topic with rates and rationale tied to the integrator, and the visualization_plan specifies five distinct plots with signal-by-signal inspection criteria. Failure_modes lists six concrete risks with specific mitigations, and repo_grounding cites the exact Euler line in diffbot_system.cpp:192, hw_positions_/hw_velocities_, on_activate NaN handling, the xacro mock-hardware switch, and yaml line numbers â€” clearly tailored to example_2's actual runtime.
